citation: Chang, C. (2024). LSHM: TTRPG Halo 'n' Horns. Google Docs.
summary:
This short Low Stress High Mayhem handout supports a streamed GameRT session of Connie Chang's micro-RPG *Halo 'n' Horns*. The document describes the game as a comedy micro-RPG for two game masters, an angel and a devil, and one mortal player whom they are trying to win over. It links to the Twitch VOD, identifies Chang and creator pages, and gathers play-alikes, read/watch-alikes, an actual-play example, safety resources, GameRT links, Twitch policy, and a brief stream outline.

The advisory structure is the most useful part of the item. The play-alikes cluster around angels, devils, deals, afterlives, and moral persuasion, while the read/watch-alikes include *Good Omens*, *The Devil Is a Part-Timer*, *The Good Place*, *The City We Became*, *American Gods*, comics, manga, anime, and television. The result is a small player-advisory record that translates a micro-RPG's tone and premise into related games and media.

The facilitation layer is also visible. The handout points to *Script Change*, *The Door Is Open*, and the TTRPG Safety Toolkit, then includes the GameRT Twitch policy emphasizing respectful conduct, no hate speech, moderator intervention, harassment reporting, and private contact. The stream outline is minimal but programmatically recognizable: tech check, introduction and rules, actual play, and discussion.

For the larger GBLS project, this item is useful as a primary example of library-adjacent online tabletop programming and advisory metadata. It shows how a short streamed RPG can be framed through creator attribution, media pathways, actual-play culture, safety tools, moderation norms, and a post-play discussion structure. These pieces could inform practical templates for low-preparation TTRPG programs or reader/player advisory records.

This source should remain outside the main literature review. It is not scholarship, not a program evaluation, and not a detailed library case report. It may be retained for a practical appendix on game advisory, streamed tabletop sessions, or safety-resource bundling, but it should not be cited as evidence for GBLS outcomes.
sourceText:
Halo ‘n’ Horns by Connie Chang
“Halo 'n' Horns is a comedy micro RPG for two GMs — an angel and a devil — and one player: the mortal they're trying to win over.”
Watch the VOD of this session here: https://www.twitch.tv/collections/tpuBAP2vGhcF7A
The Game Halo ‘n’ Horns by Connie Chang The Creator
● Connie Chang’s Twitter ● Connie Chang’s carrd.co
Play Alikes ● Heaven Has No Taste; And Other True Statements by ATLAS, gamesfromthewildwood ● Heaven Nor Hell by kiennas ● Blazing Hymn by Peach Garden Games ● To Reign in Hell by Cody Faulk ● The Last Emissary by E. R. F. Jordan ● Relics: An Angel’s Guide to Las Vegas by Tin Star Games ● Ye Who Abandon Hope, Please Sign Here by Trystan Goetze ● The Details in the Devil by Starshine Scribbles ● And I Saw Him On The Train by Riverhouse Games
Read/Watch Alikes: ● Good Omens- Terry Pratchett & Neil Gaiman (Amazon Prime & BBC2) ● The Devil is a Part-Timer- Satoshi Wagahara & Akio Hiragi (Yen Press, White Fox) ● Lucky Penny- Anath Hirsch & Yuko Ota (Oni Press) ● Sweet Paprika- Mirka Andolfo (Image) ● Heaven’s Design Team- Hebi-zou, Tsuta Suzuki, Tarako (Kodansha, Asahi Production) ● The Good Place- Michael Schur (NBC)

● The City We Became- N K Jemisin ● American Gods- Neil Gaiman (Starz)
Actual Plays ● 2+U | Halo 'n' Horns w/ Aabria Iyengar
Safety Tools ● Script Change by Beau Jágr Sheldon ● The Door is Open by Eirik Fatland ○ Blog by Lizzie Stark ○ Blog 2 by Eirik Fatland ● TTRPG Safety Toolkit collected by Kienna Shaw and Lauren Bryant-Monk ○ TTRPG Safety Toolkit Guide ○ Google Drive
Game RT Twitch Policy 1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch mod and one of us will assist you 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
Learn More About GameRT Blog: bit.ly/GameRTBlog Facebook: bit.ly/GameRTLeague & bit.ly/GameRTFacebook Resources: bit.ly/GameRTPublicResources Website: bit.ly/GameRTWebsite Questions? Email gamert@ala.org
Stream Summary Tech Check on Twitch then Go Time! Part 1: Intro and Rules Part 2: Actual Play Part 3: Discussion
